observations: Line 3 opener restated the distance-to-proximity move; cut it so the chapter starts on the village image instead of commentary.
Line 21 had a summary frame ('The village met Sylvanas in fragments') before the concrete evidence; dropped the frame.
Line 25's cup beat carried extra wording around the stain; tightened it to keep the room read lean.
Line 41 no longer carries 'Not useful yet' because Sylvanas stepping out already conveys the judgment.
Line 43 trims 'No blood worth naming' to 'No visible blood' and keeps the body absence sharper.
Line 67 drops 'No fresh sweep' because threshold dust and no rats already carry neglect.
Lines 71-73 compress the granary smell into one sentence instead of announcing the expected smell, then repeating it.
Line 89 trims Jaina's recovery beat by removing 'for a second' and the harder blink phrasing.
Line 99 reduces the track-read repetition from 'overlap/churn' into one cleaner pulse.
Line 315 trims the distance calculus by dropping the extra patrol-lines beat; the warning decision still lands.
Line 7 cut the 'Good animal' afterthought; Sylvanas's read of the mare already lands through the prior sentence.
Line 73 keeps the room-sharpening image but removes a decorative braid clause.
Line 81 shortens Jaina's bell metaphor so it explains source versus signal without doubling back.
Line 93 no longer carries the 'Useful enough' narrator stamp after Jaina's illness beat.
Lines 163-165 remove the flat-look / 'It was true' exchange so the cellar reveal keeps moving.
Line 197 trims the cellar-smell cluster and hardens 'She weighed nothing.'
Lines 203-209 remove the pre-questioning essay and the extra mage objection; Neral's compromise now does the work.
Line 229 keeps Mira's child voice but cuts the repeated 'kept saying / she said' scaffolding.
Line 285 removes 'named without naming,' which was abstract narrator language instead of field witness.
Line 397 cuts 'Not performing grief' and leaves Paret's counting behavior to speak for itself.
Line 271 changes Jaina's 'unpleasant woman' to 'woman in steel' so the joke stays inside the frightened child's frame.
Line 297 drops Jaina's 'need for poetry' jab; it sounded like an editor peeking through her mouth.
Line 393 tightens Paret's second question to 'What happened?' to keep him oral and immediate.
Line 423 changes Paret's warning from 'what won't stay down' to 'what gets back up' to keep the phrasing plain and local.
Line 81's bell explanation stayed because it is plain speech, not lab or memo language.
Line 171 Neral's rescue talk stayed clean: practical, calm, and free of therapeutic register.
Lines 355-359 keep Sylvanas and Jaina's arithmetic sniping because it reads as live barbed banter, not document voice.
Line 435 Paret's 'Scared, hungry, stupid in the usual ways' stayed because it is oral, rural, and character-specific.

changes.before: Millhaven did not look any better close.
The village met Sylvanas in fragments.
A cup on its side, long since soaked into the boards.
changes.after: A cup on its side. The stain had soaked into the boards.

changes.before: The smell should have been dry grain, mouse piss, old burlap. It was those things.
changes.after: Dry grain, mouse piss, old burlap—and under them, the wrong sweetness...

changes.before: If someone struck a bell in one room and left blood in another, you'd hear the bell first. That doesn't make the bell the wound.
changes.after: A bell struck in one room doesn't make it the wound, even if you hear it loudest.

changes.before: The air smelled of broken dirt, old sickness, and the same faint metal edge from the border.
changes.after: The air smelled of broken dirt and old sickness.

changes.before: Sylvanas gave her a flat look. 'What?' the mage murmured. 'It was true.'
She smelled of turnip cellar, fear-sweat, and old urine. She weighed almost nothing.
changes.after: She smelled of turnip cellar and fear. She weighed nothing.

changes.before: There would be no clean questioning. They still needed more than churn and empty houses.
Mam kept saying it wasn't the same fever. She said fever doesn't smell sweet.
changes.after: Mam said it wasn't fever—fever doesn't smell sweet.

changes.before: The unpleasant woman standing over us is helping too.
changes.after: The woman in steel is helping too.

changes.before: Over everything lay the same wrongness the mage had named without naming.
Does that satisfy your need for poetry?
Tell them to burn what won't stay down.
changes.after: Tell them to burn what gets back up.

changes.before: The village slid past one last time. Open door. Dead mill. Shirts on the line. A life stopped so badly the whole place had frozen around it.
changes.after: The village slid past. Open door. Dead mill. Shirts on the line still hung. The world had stopped around it.

flags: Wordcount reduction landed at 3598 from 3918 (8.2%). The draft had already gone through style-editor and style-auditor, so I kept the survivor-interrogation clarity, Paret geography, and the final southbound dread rather than forcing a blunter cut.
Five literal or load-bearing filter verbs remain in v5; they are tied to witness testimony or line-of-sight beats and did not read as POV-sanitizing sludge on this pass.
Eight uses of 'like' remain, but they are not simile-fog in dialogue-register problem spots; I left them because the actual offenders this pass were commentary layers and editorial banter.
